pi_items.source: Principles of Econometrics 5th Edition (POE5), Wooldridge. Official data definition at http://www.principlesofeconometrics.com/poe5/data/def/mroz.def
Mroz dataset variable definition - 'lfp: dummy variable = 1 if woman worked in 1975, else 0'. Confirmed from official POE5 dataset documentation.
Mroz dataset from POE5 contains 753 married women from Panel Study of Income Dynamics (PSID). Confirmed via http://www.principlesofeconometrics.com/poe5/data/csv/mroz.csv
Downloaded and analyzed actual mroz.csv dataset. Calculated: 428 / 753 = 0.5684
Mroz dataset variable list from official POE5 documentation. The dataset contains 'kidsl6' (children less than 6), NOT 'kidslt6'. Other variables (educ, exper, age) verified present in dataset.

Logit regression fit on Mroz dataset using statsmodels: logit(lfp ~ educ + exper + age + kidsl6). Education coefficient = 0.188243. Marginal effect at mean calculated as 0.0456 (from statsmodels.discrete_margins). Within tolerance.
Difference between claimed (0.054) and actual (0.0456) is 0.0084, which is less than 0.01. Tolerance claim is validated.
Analyzed mroz.csv: count(lfp==1) = 428 out of 753 total observations
Analyzed mroz.csv: count(lfp==0) = 325 out of 753 total observations

privileged_info_summary: The Mroz dataset is a real, publicly available cross-sectional labor force participation dataset containing 753 observations of married women from the Panel Study of Income Dynamics (PSID), originally compiled by T.A. Mroz (1987). The dataset contains 428 women in the labor force (56.8%). When a logit model is estimated with lfp as the dependent variable and educ, exper, age, and kidsl6 as regressors, the marginal effect of education (evaluated at the mean) is 0.0456, which rounds to 0.046 and falls within the specified tolerance of ±0.01 from the claimed value of 0.054. However, the task documentation contains a variable naming error: the correct variable name is 'kidsl6' (not 'kidslt6').
